trigger.reason: Google's overhaul of Search to prioritize AI-generated answers over traditional blue links is a major capability demo from a top-tier lab with high potential to trigger sympathy-fade selling across SEO, SaaS, and digital marketing-adjacent stocks.

primary_event.headline: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
primary_event.summary: Google has officially repositioned Search to prioritize AI-generated answers over the traditional ten blue links format. This represents a fundamental shift in how search results are presented, with most brands having minimal visibility into how AI describes them to users. The change has already triggered a measurable backlash, with DuckDuckGo installs spiking 30% as users seek alternatives.

secondary_events.headline: ElevenLabs' new music-generation model can switch genres mid-track
Meta launches Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp subscriptions with AI plans

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Google I/O 2026 announcement that AI-generated answers now front-and-center in Search, displacing the traditional ten-blue-links format, with retail extrapolating risk to SEO/SaaS/digital-marketing-adjacent names.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is genuinely disruptive for SEO/content/affiliate names, but the basket today contains mostly tickers with no real revenue overlap to Google Search distribution — cyber vuln-mgmt, healthcare analytics, cloud infra, semis. The cleanest sympathy-fade setups are DOCN, TENB, QLYS, and WK, each of which sold off in a tight panic zone with no AI-search exposure in their filings. VRRM's collapse is clearly not sympathy (govt contract reset, real impairment) and AUDC has no filings; both skipped.

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors discuss AI/ML market dynamics and competition broadly but make no mention of search-engine traffic dependency or SEO-driven customer acquisition; DOCN's self-service developer-acquisition model and IaaS/PaaS revenue have zero overlap with Google's SERP redesign. The 10-K explicitly frames AI/ML as an opportunity they are integrating (their own AI/ML offerings).
discoveries.thesis: DOCN is a developer-focused IaaS/PaaS provider. A Google Search overhaul has no meaningful pathway to harm DOCN's revenue — developers don't discover droplets via blue-link SEO. The sell-off is pure thematic 'AI bad for cloud SaaS' contagion, contradicted by the filings showing AI/ML as an embraced product line.
discoveries.what_confirms: Bounce in next 1–3 sessions as the sympathy wave passes; continued sequential ARR growth in next print; no AI/search-traffic reference in their forward commentary.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence that DOCN's marketplace or community-traffic acquisition is materially Google-search-dependent; or a hyperscaler price-war announcement that genuinely compresses cloud SMB margins.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Cloud-infrastructure names get swept into 'AI hurts SaaS' baskets on any Google/OpenAI consumer release even though their customer acquisition and revenue model have nothing to do with SERP traffic. Track this pattern across multiple AI-event days.
discoveries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DOCN is bucketed into the broad 'SaaS / cloud is AI-disrupted' thematic basket even though the trigger specifically threatens SEO and content discovery, not infrastructure.
research_pointers: Quantify DOCN's customer acquisition mix: self-service signup vs paid channels vs organic search referral
Check next earnings transcript for any mention of search-driven funnel impact
Compare DOCN's move today to prior Anthropic/OpenAI/Google capability-demo days to map the sympathy beta

discoveries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-Q explicitly states 'Advances in artificial intelligence, including models capable of rapidly identifying and exploiting software vulnerabilities, may further increase the speed and effectiveness of cyberattacks' — i.e., AI accelerates threats, raising demand for vuln-mgmt scanning. No mention of search-engine or SEO exposure anywhere. Tenable has integrated AI (ExposureAI, Tenable AI Assistant) into the product.
discoveries.thesis: Tenable is a continuous-scanning vulnerability-management platform with agent footprint across hybrid infrastructure. The Google Search overhaul cannot touch any part of its revenue model. The filings actively argue AI-enhanced attacks INCREASE demand for their product. The sell-off is generic 'AI is bad for security software' contagion.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable renewal commentary in next quarter, no degradation in NRR, continued enterprise platform momentum.
discoveries.what_kills: Evidence a hyperscaler-bundled AI security tool is taking share at enterprise level; or weak Q2 print on its own merits.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Cyber vuln-mgmt names get tagged as 'AI replaces scanning' on every capability demo even though their own filings describe AI as a tailwind to the threat landscape they monetize.
discoveries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Cyber-security software is being lumped into the broad 'AI commoditizes enterprise software' panic, despite zero overlap with Search.
research_pointers: Track TENB's net retention rate trend Q-over-Q for any signs of real AI-displacement
Compare TENB and QLYS moves on AI-event days vs their own earnings days to isolate sympathy beta
Check whether AI-generated exploit news cycles correlate with vuln-mgmt bookings acceleration

discoveries.name: Qualys, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors note AI/ML technologies are incorporated into Qualys products and that 'AI/machine learning technologies may be used in connection with certain cybersecurity attacks, resulting in heightened risks of security breaches' — framing AI as a demand driver, not a substitute. No SEO or Search exposure. Compliance/PCI/HIPAA audit trail is the moat the curation flags.
discoveries.thesis: Qualys runs continuous scanning and compliance-grade reporting (PCI, HIPAA, audit trail) — none of which Google Search displaces. Filings show AI integrated into the product and AI-driven threats as a tailwind to demand. Sympathy drop is pure sector contagion.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable renewal rates, continued compliance-segment growth, AI security feature adoption noted on next call.
discoveries.what_kills: A major customer publicly migrating off Qualys to a hyperscaler-native scanner; weak guide on its own merit.
discoveries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: QLYS is in the broad 'AI replaces enterprise security' thematic bucket, no actual exposure to Search-result distribution.
research_pointers: Examine QLYS compliance-module revenue trajectory (PCI, HIPAA) as a stickiness proxy
Compare QLYS and TENB co-movement on AI capability-demo days vs their idiosyncratic days

discoveries.name: Workiva Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K notes >35% of revenue from SEC filings, with XBRL tagging, SOX, capital markets, multi-entity, ESG reporting as core. AI is mentioned only as a general competitive/technology risk; the product is the regulatory-grade audit trail and Big-4 auditor workflow integration. No Google Search dependency.
discoveries.thesis: Workiva's moat is the auditor-signed-off, XBRL-tagged, SOX-compliant report — AI can draft text but cannot replace the audit trail or regulatory submission workflow. Google Search redesign is entirely orthogonal. The sell-off is generic vertical-SaaS contagion.
discoveries.what_confirms: Renewal rate >94% sustained, continued non-SEC bookings growth, no customer-cited AI displacement.
discoveries.what_kills: A Big-4 auditor adopting an LLM-native reporting tool that bypasses Workiva; CSRD-driven demand collapse confirmed in next print (a real, non-AI risk already in the filings).
discoveries.what_to_learn: Regulated-workflow SaaS (SOX, XBRL, audit) repeatedly gets sold on 'AI writes reports now' panics, but the audit trail/regulator-grade output is the actual product, not the text generation.
discoveries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: WK swept into the 'AI obsoletes vertical SaaS workflows' thematic basket despite zero overlap with the specific Search-distribution capability being demoed.
research_pointers: Monitor CSRD scope amendments (real, non-AI risk flagged in the 10-K) for actual demand impact
Check WK's net retention and SOX/audit-segment growth in next print

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly states 'infrastructure investments in artificial intelligence (AI) have increased substantially, which is driving significant demand increases in the Data Center Computing market' — AEIS is a direct AI capex beneficiary providing power conversion to data centers and semi fabs. No Search/SEO exposure.
discoveries.thesis: AEIS sells precision power conversion to semi fabs and data centers — both direct beneficiaries of AI buildout. The filings literally cite AI as a demand driver. A Google Search redesign has no pathway to AEIS revenue. The drop is generic semi-cap/capex-peak-fear contagion that happens to coincide with the AI headline.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued Data Center Computing segment growth in next print; affirmed AI-driven capex commentary.
discoveries.what_kills: Confirmed hyperscaler capex cut announcement; gross margin compression in Data Center segment that suggests pricing pressure.
discoveries.catalyst: Google I/O 2026: AI-generated answers now front and center in Search, replacing traditional blue links
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Semi/picks-shovels names occasionally trade lower on AI capability demos as part of 'capex peak' fears, but AEIS's exposure here is sympathy-only.
research_pointers: Track hyperscaler capex guidance from MSFT/GOOGL/META/AMZN for any actual cut signal
Monitor AEIS Data Center segment QoQ growth and margin
5-day chart is still positive — confirm panic zone is intraday rather than week-long trend

skipped.reason: 70% one-day collapse is not sympathy-fade — filings reveal a materially changed NYCDOT contract, a major Commercial Services customer in short-term contract extension/renegotiation (10%+ of revenue), and Ontario banned automated speed enforcement. Real impairment, not panic.
Both 10-K and 10-Q Risk Factors unavailable — cannot do filings-based threat assessment. Also expected_role is real_victim_control; today's move is positive (+3.74%) so no buy signal anyway.

Edge-AI inference SoCs are a direct beneficiary per filings (deep fusion, VLMs, LLMs on edge); however, 5-day is +18% and today is only -3.2% on elevated volume — looks like normal profit-taking on an extended move, not panic-zone sympathy. Not a clean fade setup.
Move is positive (+3.17%) — no negative panic to fade. Also concurrent CEO transition and Project Nexus restructuring make catalysts non-clean.
